title: Refactor deeply nested conditional with pinning across args
prompt:
Given:

```
def can_transfer?(from_user, to_user, post) do
  if from_user.id == post.owner_id do
    if to_user.role == "editor" or to_user.role == "admin" do
      true
    else
      false
    end
  else
    false
  end
end
```

Rewrite as two function heads. The true clause uses pinning: `def can_transfer?(%{id: id}, %{role: role}, %{owner_id: id}) when role in ["editor", "admin"], do: true`. The fallback returns `false`. Note the pinning of `id` across the first and third arguments. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.PostTransfer do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Cross-arg pinning + role-in-list guard composition. Rarely seen even in human-written Elixir.
